chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The one for whom there is no wergild

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَعْنَفَ عَلَى امْرَأَتِهِ أَوِ امْرَأَةٍ أَعْنَفَتْ عَلَى زَوْجِهَا فَقَتَلَ أَحَدُهُمَا الْآخَرَ قَالَ لَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْهِمَا إِذَا كَانَا مَأْمُونَيْنِ فَإِنِ اتُّهِمَا أَلْزَمَهُمَا الْيَمِينَ بِاللَّهِ أَنَّهُمَا لَمْ يُرِيدَا الْقَتْلَ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Salih Bin Saeed, from Yunus, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who was harsh upon his wife, or a woman who was harsh upon her husband. So one of the two killed the other. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon both of them when they both trusted each other. So if they were to complain, it would necessitate them to swear an oath with Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, that they did not intend the killing’.
